root_text.sn56.43:1.2: Tattha yaṁ kiñci cakkhunā rūpaṁ passati, aniṭṭharūpaññeva passati no iṭṭharūpaṁ; akantarūpaññeva passati no kantarūpaṁ; amanāparūpaññeva passati no manāparūpaṁ.
root_text.sn56.43:1.5: yaṁ kiñci manasā dhammaṁ vijānāti, aniṭṭharūpaññeva vijānāti no iṭṭharūpaṁ; akantarūpaññeva vijānāti no kantarūpaṁ; amanāparūpaññeva vijānāti no manāparūpan”ti.
root_text.sn56.43:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn56.43:2.2: “mahā vata so, bhante, pariḷāho, sumahā vata so, bhante, pariḷāho.
root_text.sn56.43:2.3: Atthi nu kho, bhante, etamhā pariḷāhā añño pariḷāho mahantataro ceva bhayānakataro cā”ti?
root_text.sn56.43:2.4: “Atthi kho, bhikkhu, etamhā pariḷāhā añño pariḷāho mahantataro ca bhayānakataro cā”ti.
root_text.sn56.43:3.1: “Katamo pana, bhante, etamhā pariḷāhā añño pariḷāho mahantataro ca bhayānakataro cā”ti?
root_text.sn56.43:3.2: “Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti …pe…
root_text.sn56.43:3.3: ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti, te jātisaṁvattanikesu saṅkhāresu abhiramanti …pe…
root_text.sn56.43:3.5: abhisaṅkharitvā jātipariḷāhenapi pariḍayhanti, jarāpariḷāhenapi pariḍayhanti, maraṇapariḷāhenapi pariḍayhanti, sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsapariḷāhenapi pariḍayhanti.
root_text.sn56.43:3.6: Te na parimuccanti jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi.
root_text.sn56.43:4.1: Ye ca kho keci, bhikkhave, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti.
root_text.sn56.43:4.4: anabhisaṅkharitvā jātipariḷāhenapi na pariḍayhanti, jarāpariḷāhenapi na pariḍayhanti, maraṇapariḷāhenapi na pariḍayhanti, sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsapariḷāhenapi na pariḍayhanti.
root_text.sn56.43:4.5: Te parimuccanti jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi.
root_text.sn56.43:5.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.43:1.1: “Mendicants, there is a hell called ‘The Mighty Fever’.
translation_text.sn56.43:1.2: There, whatever sight you see with your eye is unlikable, not likable; undesirable, not desirable; unpleasant, not pleasant.
translation_text.sn56.43:1.3: Whatever sound you hear … Whatever odor you smell … Whatever flavor you taste …
translation_text.sn56.43:1.5: Whatever idea you know with your mind is unlikable, not likable; undesirable, not desirable; unpleasant, not pleasant.”
translation_text.sn56.43:2.1: When he said this, one of the mendicants said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn56.43:2.2: “Sir, that fever really is mighty, so very mighty.
translation_text.sn56.43:2.3: Is there any other fever more mighty and terrifying than this one?”
translation_text.sn56.43:3.2: “Mendicants, there are ascetics and brahmins who don’t truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.43:3.3: They take pleasure in choices that lead to rebirth …
translation_text.sn56.43:3.4: They continue to make such choices …
translation_text.sn56.43:3.5: Having made such choices, they burn with the fever of rebirth, old age, and death, of sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn56.43:3.6: They’re not freed from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn56.43:3.7: They’re not freed from suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn56.43:4.1: There are ascetics and brahmins who truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.43:4.2: They don’t take pleasure in choices that lead to rebirth …
translation_text.sn56.43:4.4: Having stopped making such choices, they don’t burn with the fever of rebirth, old age, and death, of sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn56.43:4.5: They’re freed from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn56.43:4.6: They’re freed from suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn56.43:5.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
